title: My plan to become objectively the smartest person EVER within a year by passing history tests. Like?

enrichment.summary: Kyle proposes replacing IQ tests with a history memorization test as the objective measure of intelligence, arguing that history knowledge is more grounded in real-world reasoning than abstract IQ puzzles. He outlines a scoring system where passing his 150-event England timeline test multiple times maps to progressively higher IQ equivalents, with his personal goal of completing it 10-11 times to declare himself the smartest person in the world.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Not a valid measure of intelligence — too abstract and not grounded in reality
Money should not be the default measure of smartness, though culture treats it that way
Scoring well on history tests is the best objective measure of intelligence
Fluid intelligence enables crystallized intelligence, so high IQ and history memory are linked
Plans to declare himself the smartest person ever after passing his England history test 11 times
